data.title: [ccc3-954a] RED: Write unit tests for migrate-markdown-refs.py covering runtime vs prose classification
data.description:
## What to do
Create tests/scripts/test-migrate-markdown-refs.py — failing (RED) unit tests for migrate-markdown-refs.py before it exists.

## Tests to write
- test_runtime_convert_code_block: plugins/dso path inside ```bash...``` fenced block → ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/...
- test_runtime_convert_read_path: path after "Read(" or "Grep on" → ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/...
- test_prose_strip_prefix: prose backtick like `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/foo.md` used as cross-reference description → `docs/contracts/foo.md`
- test_shim_exempt_untouched: lines with `# shim-exempt:` annotation are NOT modified
- test_plugin_self_ref_ok_untouched: lines with `# plugin-self-ref-ok:` annotation are NOT modified
- test_dry_run_no_mutation: --dry-run flag makes no file changes
- test_idempotent: running twice produces same result as once

## Classification heuristic
Runtime context (→ ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/):
  - Lines inside fenced code blocks (```bash, ```shell, ```)
  - Lines matching: Read\(, Grep.*plugins/dso, Bash.*plugins/dso
  - Lines starting with: cat , source , bash , sh 
Prose context (→ strip plugins/dso/ prefix):
  - Backtick paths in flowing prose sentences (not in code blocks)
  - Description text, not imperative tool calls

## Files to create
- tests/scripts/test-migrate-markdown-refs.py

## testing_mode: RED
